• If the target is a non-**HERO CAVALRY** unit, return 1 slain model to it.
• If the target is a **HERO** or **MONSTER**, **Heal (D3)** the target.

warscrolls.loadout: This unit comprises 1 Soul Shepherd and 1 War Surgeon.
warscrolls.notes: This unit cannot be reinforced. This unit can only be taken in a regiment that contains a Freeguild Command Corp Adjutant unit and no other Freeguild Command Corp Auxiliaries. This unit does not count towards the number of friendly units that can accompany a HERO.

***Tactical Bombing:*** Roll a D3. On a 2+, inflict an amount of mortal damage on the target equal to the roll.

***Drop It All!:*** Make 2 rolls of D3. For each 2+, inflict an amount of mortal damage on the target equal to the roll. Then, roll a D3 for each unit (friendly and enemy) within the target’s combat range that does not have **FLY**. On a 2+, inflict an amount of mortal damage on that unit equal to the roll. Once the ‘Drop It All!’ effect of this ability has been resolved, this unit cannot use this ability again for the rest of the battle.

abilities.phaseDetails: Once Per Turn (Army), Your Movement Phase

abilities.effect:
If there are no enemy units contesting the target objective, it is considered by you to be **consecrated**. Otherwise, roll a dice. On a 3+, the target objective is considered by you to be **consecrated**.

While they are wholly within 6" of a **consecrated** objective and **under orders**:
• Friendly non-**WAR MACHINE SIGMARITE** units have **WARD (5+)**.
• Friendly **SIGMARITE WAR MACHINES** have **WARD (6+)**.

If your opponent gains control of a **consecrated** objective, it is no longer **consecrated**.

armiesOfRenown.name: Allies of the Free Cities
armiesOfRenown.lore:
The free cities of the God-King stand as bulwarks against the brutality of the daemon, the gheist and the orruk. Founded in an era of rekindled hope, these vast, fortified strongholds are interspersed throughout the Mortal Realms, each home to a bustling population of faithful citizens.

When you pick the Cities of Sigmar faction for your army, you can choose for it to be an Allies of the Free Cities Army of Renown. If you do so, use the faction rules on these pages instead of the Cities of Sigmar faction rules. Every unit in your army must be drawn from the units listed as described in Army of Renown: Allies of the Free Cities. All units in your army gain the **ALLIES OF THE FREE CITIES** keyword.

rules.name: Form up, Allies!
rules.declare:
Pick a friendly **ALLIES OF THE FREE CITIES** unit to use this ability, then pick 2 other visible friendly **ALLIES OF THE FREE CITIES** units wholly within 12" of it to be the targets.

The unit using this ability and the targets:
• cannot be **under orders**.
• must each be a different unit type.
rules.effect:
For the rest of the battle round, the unit using this ability and the targets are **under orders**.

**Designer’s Note:** *‘Unit type’ refers to the name of the warscroll (Core Rules, 4.0), e.g. ‘Freeguild Cavalier-Marshal’ or ‘Allies of the Free Cities Auric Hearthguard’.*

rules.name: Forces of Order
rules.effect:
While 3 friendly **ALLIES OF THE FREE CITIES** units are wholly within 12" of each other and 1 of those units has the **AELF** keyword, 1 of those units has the **DUARDIN** keyword and 1 of those units has the **SIGMARITE** keyword:

• Those units have **WARD (5+)** against shooting attacks.
• Add 3 to those units’ control scores.
• Add 1 to wound rolls for those units’ combat attacks.

artefacts.phaseDetails: Once Per Battle (Army), Any Hero Phase
artefacts.declare: Pick this unit and each visible friendly **ALLIES OF THE FREE CITIES** unit wholly within 18" of this unit to be the targets.
artefacts.effect:
For the rest of the turn:

• The targets are **under orders**.
• The targets have **WARD (6+)**.
• The targets have a maximum control score of 1.

heroicTraits.effect: If you roll a 1-3 when determining the distance that unit can move, you can use a value of 4 instead.

spells.effect: For the rest of the turn, melee weapons used by friendly **ALLIES OF THE FREE CITIES** units have **Charge (+1 Damage)** for attacks that target enemy units that are contesting the target objective.

spells.effect: If the chanting roll was 10+, you can pick another eligible unit to be a second target. Until the start of your next turn, enemy units have a maximum control score of 1 while they are in combat with any of the targets.

armiesOfRenown.name: The Iron March
armiesOfRenown.lore:
Cannons roar and the gun-studded walking castles known as Cogforts lumber forwards, their every step an earthquake and their every barrage gouging gaping holes in the enemy line. Once, these engines of war were used to anchor the ever-expanding borders of Sigmar’s empire. Now First Marshal Tahlia Vedra has ordered them forward en masse, risking her most precious assets to blunt the enemy’s surge and throw them back. Such bold actions might be humanity’s only chance to snatch triumph from the precipice of catastrophe.

When you pick the Cities of Sigmar faction for your army, you can choose for it to be the Iron March Army of Renown. If you do so, use the faction rules on these pages instead of the Cities of Sigmar faction rules. Every unit in your army must be drawn from the units listed as described in Army of Renown: The Iron March. All units in your army gain the **IRON MARCH** keyword.

• must each be a different unit type.
rules.effect:
For the rest of the battle round, the unit using this ability and the targets are **under orders**.

**Designer’s Note:** *‘Unit type’ refers to the name of the warscroll (Core Rules, 4.0), e.g. ‘Cannonade Cogfort’ or ‘Mallus Forgepriest’.*

battleTraits.phaseDetails: Once Per Turn (Army), Your Shooting Phase
battleTraits.declare: Pick a friendly unit that is **under orders** to use this ability.
battleTraits.effect: Pick 1 of that unit’s ranged weapons. Add 1 to hit rolls for shooting attacks made with that weapon for the rest of the turn.

battleTraits.name: Sound the Attack!
battleTraits.phaseDetails: Once Per Turn (Army), Reaction: You declared a CHARGE ability for a unit that is under orders
battleTraits.effect: You can change the lowest D6 in the charge roll to match the highest D6.

battleTraits.name: Into Them, You Dogs!
battleTraits.phaseDetails: Once Per Turn (Army), Any Combat Phase
battleTraits.declare: Pick a friendly **HERO** that is **under orders** to use this ability. That unit is the **field officer** for the rest of the turn. Then, pick 2 other visible friendly non‑**HERO** units that are **under orders** and wholly within 12" of the **field officer** to be the targets.
battleTraits.effect: For the rest of the turn, the first time the **field officer** uses a **FIGHT** ability, you can pick a target that has not used a **FIGHT** ability this turn. That target is **inspirited** and the other target is **emboldened**. The **inspirited** target can be picked to use a **FIGHT** ability immediately after the **FIGHT** ability used by the **field officer** has been resolved. Then, if the **emboldened** target has not used a **FIGHT** ability this turn, it can be picked to use a **FIGHT** ability immediately after the **inspirited** target’s **FIGHT** ability has been resolved.

battleTraits.name: Hurl Them Back!
battleTraits.phaseDetails: Once Per Turn (Army), Any Combat Phase
battleTraits.declare: Pick a friendly unit that is **under orders** and in combat with an enemy unit that charged this turn to use this ability.
battleTraits.effect: For the rest of the turn, add 1 to the Rend characteristic of that unit’s melee weapons.
